title: Add bin/install.sh bootstrap script for fresh Ubuntu 24.04
userStory: As an operator, I want one curl-pipe command to take a brand-new VPS to a running brainbot so that re-provisioning is not a day-long re-discovery exercise.
acceptanceCriteria: New file bin/install.sh, designed to be safe under `curl ... | bash`
Idempotent: re-running on an already-installed box is a no-op (or a deploy)

Steps: install docker + docker compose plugin if missing, install git + rclone if missing, prompt for or accept env vars (BRAIN_DOMAIN, BRAIN_BEARER_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, etc.) — accepts via stdin or pre-set env, never echoed
Clones brainbot to /opt/brainbot if absent, otherwise pulls
Writes /opt/brainbot/compose/.env from prompted values if .env does not exist; never overwrites an existing .env
Sets up a systemd unit or cron entry to run bin/deploy.sh on a schedule only if user opts in (e.g. `BRAINBOT_AUTO_UPDATE=1`); default is manual
Verify: on a fresh Hostinger Ubuntu 24.04 VPS, `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/<user>/brainbot/main/bin/install.sh | bash` produces a healthy brainbot stack with no further commands
Verify: re-running the same curl on the same box does not duplicate state and ends in a successful deploy
